task: Launch-quality advanced task: Silicon Photonics Ring Resonator Trim Plan. Reality context: a photonics transceiver lot has wavelength drift after packaging cure. Embedded packet: metric map: metric1=resonance_error_pm, metric2=heater_mW, metric3=q_factor_k, metric4=crosstalk_dB; records: R1 resonance_error_pm=25.9 heater_mW=70.9 q_factor_k=27.8 crosstalk_dB=-15.3; R2 resonance_error_pm=3.4 heater_mW=70.9 q_factor_k=92.4 crosstalk_dB=-24.8; R3 resonance_error_pm=39.8 heater_mW=70.4 q_factor_k=62.9 crosstalk_dB=-30.9; R4 resonance_error_pm=20.8 heater_mW=36.2 q_factor_k=114.5 crosstalk_dB=-34.1; limit_resonance_error_pm=40.4; limit_heater_mW=53.6; limit_q_factor_k=59.3; decision_budget_usd=115000; sensitivity_shift_pct=5; hard_threshold_record_count=1; removed_context="certification workflow expected reproducible calculations and signoff rationale". Rules and constraints: Use worst-case scoring: normalize each metric to the embedded maximum, apply weights 0.40, 0.30, 0.20, 0.10, and classify the top quartile as immediate action. Options or recommendations cannot use external measurements, specialized licensed software, or hidden assumptions; all calculations must use only the embedded packet, and any contradictory or under-supported record must be marked needs_review. Required work: compute trim settings and reject unstable channels; calculate derived metrics, classify every record, identify the smallest defensible intervention, and write the decision rationale. Deliver ring_trim_plan.csv + photonics_memo.md with formulas or executable logic, record-level citations, validation checks, sensitivity notes, and a final recommendation table suitable for expert review.
